title: quercus glandulifera var. japonica
intro: Quercus glandulifera var. japonica, commonly known as Japanese pedunculate oak, is a variety of deciduous tree native to East Asia, belonging to the Quercus genus. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, as well as its ability to lower cholesterol levels and reduce blood pressure. The health benefits of Japanese pedunculate oak include reducing the risk of cardiovascular disease, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system. Japanese pedunculate oak is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to reap its health benefits.
